description: The philosophical foundation, political ideology, and direct appeal to citizens framing the TVK 2026 Manifesto.

sections.title: Preface (முன்னுரை)
key_points: Adopts Thiruvalluvar's conceptual framework of Aram (Ethics), Porul (Economy/Wealth), and Inbam (Happiness/Joy) as the basis for good governance [1, 2].
Emphasizes that Aram, Porul, and Inbam are not sequential steps, but interconnected duties that must be implemented simultaneously [2].

Asserts that wealth without ethics is merely good intentions without action, and an administration ultimately fails if its people remain unhealthy, uneducated, or unhappy despite a strong economy [2].

sections.title: Why is Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam here? (தமிழக வெற்றிக் கழகம் ஏன் இங்கே உள்ளது?)
key_points: Core Ideological Principle: Built entirely on the principle of 'Equality for all by birth' (பிறப்பொக்கும் எல்லா உயிர்க்கும்) [3].
Positioned as a secular social justice movement dedicated to pure, uncompromising politics and breaking systemic corruption and hereditary political dominance [3].
Critique of the State Government: Accuses the ruling party of hiding family-driven wealth accumulation and corruption behind the shield of the Dravidian movement and Periyar's name [3].
Critique of the Union Government: Strongly opposes the 'One Language, One Election, One Identity' push, identifying it as a direct attack on state autonomy and federalism [3].
Financial Transparency: Highlights the severe financial reality that Tamil Nadu's outstanding debt is projected to cross ₹10.71 lakh crores by 2026-27 [4].
Honest Administration: Promises honest fiscal management inspired by former Chief Minister Kamarajar [4].
Political Purpose: States the party entered politics not to enjoy power, but to correct systemic flaws and implement a ready, costed governance plan [4].

sections.title: A Final Appeal to Voters (வாக்காளர்களுக்கு ஓர் வேண்டுகோள்)
key_points: Highlights the severe current state of governance, describing it as 'Commission-Collection-Corruption', which actively harms youth, farmers, fishermen, and MSMEs [5].
Vision for 'Victory Tamil Nadu' (வெற்றித் தமிழ்நாடு): Promises a state free of drugs, poverty, and corruption; ensuring equality, world-class education, healthcare, and safety for women [6].

Ideological Inspiration: Draws on the wisdom of Thiruvalluvar, the social justice of Periyar, the honest administration of Kamarajar, the social empowerment of Ambedkar, and the bravery of Rani Velu Nachiyar [6].
Direct Call to Action: Urges citizens to vote for the party on April 23 by pressing the button corresponding to the 'Whistle' (விசில்) symbol to establish a true people-centric government [7].
